figures.caption: Figure 2: A flowchart representing the research pipeline for the project

Figure 5: Correlation analysis between the lightness (V) of the abdomen and the latitude in Figure 5a, as well as the hour of day in Figure 5b. The x-axis corresponds to the latitude, and the hour, while the y-axis corresponds to the lightness of the abdomen. The higher the value, the closer the colour was to white, while the lower the value, the closer the colour was to black. As observed from both graphs, there is a slight negative correlation with the lightness of the abdomen.
Table 9: A table containing the count of responses by each computer scientist. This has been aggregated over 150 images, of various complexities.

Table 2: Performance of the model is documented for the bounding boxes, i.e., identification of the Odonate, as part of Stage 1 experiments. Training and testing was done on the first version of the dataset [26], as discussed in Section 4. The metrics are recorded for the mean average precisions and average precision for each class, and the best performing model is in bold.

Figure 4: Extraction of colour and dominant hues using K-Means Clustering. The final resultant image is a combination of multiple panels: the original image and the prediction from the model. The other panels contain each identified part and the palette of dominant hues.
Table 7: Range of responses in the survey

Table 8: A table containing the count of responses by each entomologist. This has been aggregated over ≈ 150 images and omitting missing responses. The 150 images were random, and contained different complexities and background conditions.
Figure 12: Inferences run by the trained models on the second version of the dataset, at 150 epochs
Figure 6: Zero-shot learning on current implementations and models

Table 4: Performance of the model is documented for the bounding boxes i.e., identification of the Odonate, as part of Stage 2 experiments. Training and testing was done on the second version of the dataset [28], as discussed in Section 5.1.2. The metrics are recorded for the mean average precisions and average precision for each class, and the best performing model is highlighted in grey.

Table 3: Performance of the model is documented for the masks i.e., segmentation of the parts of the Odonate, as part of Stage 1 experiments. Training and testing was done on the first version of the dataset [26], as discussed in Section 4. The metrics are recorded for the mean average precisions and average precision for each class, and the best performing model is in bold.

Figure 1: Manual annotation of dragonfly and resulting segmentation mask from QuPath. The first image is the original image and the second image is the annotated image, which has been exported as .TIFF file
Figure 11: Inferences run by the trained Mask2Former model at different epochs.
Figure 9: Inferences run by the trained MaskRCNN model at different epochs.
Figure 10: Inferences run by the trained MaskDINO model at different epochs.

Table 5: Performance of the model is documented for the masks i.e., segmentation of the Odonate, as part of Stage 2 experiments. Training and testing is done on the second version of the dataset [28], as discussed in Section 5.1.2. The metrics are recorded for the mean average precisions and average precision for each class, and the best performing model is in bold.
Figure 3: Inference of trained YOLO on an unseen image after two rounds of fine-tuning on the dataset.

Figure 8: Correlation analysis between the mean lightness (V) values and the hour of day. The x-axis corresponds to the adjusted hour of the day, and the y-axis corresponds to the mean lightness value of the body part. The higher the value, the closer the colour was to white, and the lower the value, the closer to black. As observed from both graphs, there is a slight negative correlation between the body part and the lightness

Figure 7: Correlation analysis between the mean lightness (V) values and the latitude. The x-axis corresponds to the latitude, and the y-axis corresponds to the mean lightness value of the body part. The higher the value, the closer the colour was to white, and the lower the value, the closer to black. As observed from both graphs, there is a slight negative correlation between the body part and the lightness
